add.correctness_coverage: Torch/AscendC FULL; PyPTO B=1 only persisted
add.limitation: PyPTO correctness B=2..64 not persisted to JSON

div.profiler_coverage: Torch+AscendC msprof B=1..32; PyPTO not profiled
div.rc2_fix: tile_shape(1024,2048) was too large; changed to (128,1024). Dtype mismatch in test also fixed. All 6 batches pass bitwise (max_abs=0.0).

equal.rc2_fix: Two root causes: (1) output was DT_FP16 instead of DT_BOOL (packed bitmask), (2) BOOL output requires ta≤64 in tile shape. All 7 batches pass bitwise.

not.correctness_coverage: Torch PASS; AscendC PASS (42/42 cases); PyPTO PASS
not.limitation: Profiler is Event-based, not msprof. Not comparable with arithmetic.

or.correctness_coverage: Torch PASS; AscendC PASS (49/49 cases); PyPTO PASS (bitwise_or for 0/1)
or.limitation: PyPTO uses bitwise_or (no logical_or API in framework). Correct for bool 0/1 inputs. Profiler Event-based.

where.rc2_fix: uint8 condition → backend TiledWhereOperation ExpandFunction bug. Workaround: convert uint8→bool in wrapper (DT_BOOL condition). All 7 batches pass bitwise.

expand.profiler_coverage: FULL — msprof all 3 routes (repeat=3, warmup=200, loops=100)
expand.limitation: PyPTO uses per-row AICPU dispatch (~3000 us, not compute kernel). Not comparable. AscendC is TRUE_DEVICE_IMPLEMENTATION (Duplicate).

transpose.profiler_coverage: FULL — msprof Torch+AscendC (B=1..64, repeat=5); PyPTO not profiled
transpose.rc2_fix: tile_shape(128,1024) was too large. Changed to (64,256). All shapes up to 2048×2048 pass bitwise.
transpose.rc2_ascendc_perf: Optimized kernel: tile size 32×32 (up from 16×16) + double buffering. ~13-18% improvement.

reduce_sum.correctness_coverage: Torch 62/70 PASS; AscendC 21/70 PASS; PyPTO 21/70 PASS
reduce_sum.profiler_coverage: FULL — msprof Torch+AscendC (B=1..64, repeat=5); PyPTO not profiled
reduce_sum.limitation: FP16 accumulation for 384-element reduction exceeds atol=0.01 (max_abs~0.03). AscendC and PyPTO both use native FP16 accum. Torch uses FP32 accum.

pypto.limitation: FP16 accumulation causes max_abs ~0.015-0.031. Not bitwise.
pypto.rc2_fix: Manual set_cube_tile_shapes([16,32],[16,32],[16,32]) workaround. All shapes compile and run.

matmul.profiler_coverage: FULL (Torch + Ascend C msprof; PyPTO N/A)

rc1_fixes.fix_profiler_parser: primary_compute_kernel_us now correctly returns KERNEL_MIX_AIC for PyPTO instead of KERNEL_AICPU executor
rc1_fixes.fix_div_correctness: B=4,8,16,32 reference files existed; re-ran correctness. All 6 batches PASS now.
rc1_fixes.fix_relu_torch_correctness: torch/correctness_results.json was missing; generated. All 7 batches PASS.
rc1_fixes.fix_reduce_sum_parsed: 14 parsed JSON files generated from existing msprof raw data (was missing)
rc1_fixes.fix_performance_csv: All values now use primary_compute_kernel_us consistently

rc2_fixes.fix_matmul_pypto: Manual set_cube_tile_shapes([16,32],[16,32],[16,32]) workaround unblocked PyPTO MatMul. All shapes compile and run. FP16 accum causes max_abs~0.015-0.031.
rc2_fixes.fix_div_pypto: tile_shape(1024,2048) too large → changed to (128,1024). Dtype mismatch in test fixed. All 6 batches pass bitwise.
rc2_fixes.fix_where_pypto: uint8 condition→backend ExpandFunction bug. Workaround: convert uint8→bool in wrapper. All 7 batches pass bitwise.
rc2_fixes.fix_transpose_pypto: tile_shape(128,1024) too large → changed to (64,256). All shapes up to 2048×2048 pass bitwise.
rc2_fixes.fix_equal_pypto: Output was DT_FP16 instead of DT_BOOL. BOOL output requires ta≤64. Both fixed. All 7 batches pass bitwise.
rc2_fixes.fix_transpose_ascendc_perf: Optimized kernel: tile size 32×32 + double buffering. ~13-18% improvement across all batches.

known_limitations.description: Uses bitwise_or (no logical_or API). Correct for 0/1 bool.
FP16 accum precision exceeds atol=0.01 for 384-element reduction
max_abs ~0.015-0.031 due to FP16 accumulation (not bitwise)
Per-row AICPU dispatch ~3000 us — not a compute kernel
Correctness B=2..64 not persisted to JSON

ascendc_implementation_audit.note: All 11 operators have genuine Ascend C device-side kernels verified by source code AND correctness runs on NPU.

rc2_phase1_completeness.parser_traceability: 9 bugs identified; needs per-iteration stats and provenance
rc2_phase1_completeness.sha256: All 12 operators have valid SHA256SUMS (8 were fixed)
